2605.19940.title: Robotics-Inspired Guardrails for Foundation Models in Socially Sensitive Domains
2605.19940.paper_summary: Foundation models are increasingly deployed in socially sensitive domains such as education, mental health, and caregiving, where failures are often cumulative and context-dependent. Existing guardrail approaches -- ranging from training-time alignment to prompting, decoding constraints, and post-hoc moderation -- primarily provide empirical risk reduction rather than enforceable behavioral guarantees, and largely treat safety as a property of individual outputs rather than interaction trajectories. We reframe guardrails as a problem of runtime behavioral control over interaction trajectories, drawing on robotics to introduce formal constructs for constraint enforcement in uncertain, closed-loop systems. We instantiate these ideas in the Grounded Observer framework and apply it across three real-world deployments: small talk, in-home autism therapy, and behavioral de-escalation in schools. Across settings, the framework enables runtime interventions that mitigate drift into undesirable interaction regimes while adapting to diverse social contexts. We discuss extensions to the framework and propose research directions toward stronger guarantees.
2605.19940.paper_summary_zh: 基礎模型越來越多地應用於社會敏感領域，如教育、心理健康和護理，這些領域的失敗往往是累積性的且依賴於上下文。現有的防護措施——從訓練時的對齊到提示、解碼約束和事後調節——主要提供經驗風險降低，而不是可執行的行為保證，並且在很大程度上將安全視為單個輸出的屬性，而不是互動軌跡。我們將防護措施重新定義為對互動軌跡的運行時行為控制問題，借鑒機器人技術引入不確定的閉環系統中約束執行的正式構造。我們在基於實地觀察者的框架中實現這些想法，並將其應用於三個現實世界的部署：小型對話、家庭自閉症治療和學校中的行為降級。在各種環境中，該框架實現了運行時干預，減少了向不良互動範疇的漂移，同時適應多樣的社會背景。我們討論了對該框架的擴展，並提出了加強保證的研究方向。
2605.19940.author: Rebecca Ramnauth et.al.
2605.19940.authors: Rebecca Ramnauth, Drazen Brscic, Brian Scassellati
